literature.signal: B7-H4 protein expression confirmed in 30–45% TNBC tumor samples by IHC
B7-H4 mRNA overexpression correlated with basal-like subtype in TCGA breast cancer dataset
Normal tissue B7-H4 expression restricted primarily to epithelial cells of uterus and kidney
B7-H4 internalization confirmed upon antibody binding in TNBC cell lines
No validated IHC companion diagnostic or prospective biomarker cutoff study published

trials.signal: AZD9401 (AZ/FirstBio) Phase I dose escalation ongoing; TNBC cohort active
SGN-B7H4V (Seagen/Pfizer) Phase I in TNBC and other B7-H4+ tumors
2 additional preclinical B7-H4 ADC programs from mid-size biotechs advancing toward IND
Sacituzumab govitecan (Trodelvy) approved TNBC 2L+ — sets clinical bar
TDXd DESTINY-Breast04/06 approved HER2-low/ultralow BC — further narrows unselected TNBC population

regulatory.signal: TNBC accelerated approval via ORR endpoint is established precedent (SG)
Biomarker-defined subgroup approval adds companion diagnostic requirement and development complexity
No B7-H4 companion diagnostic cleared; IHC assay development adds 18–24 months

commercial.signal: TNBC US incidence ~45,000/year; B7-H4+ subgroup est. 13,500–18,000 patients
SG 2L+ TNBC market ~$800M US revenue (2023); sets pricing floor
High competitive saturation: 2 approved ADCs + 4 B7-H4 programs + multiple other ADC entrants
Payer differentiation requires biomarker-defined label; unselected approval unlikely to achieve premium pricing

literature.signal: Tissue Factor expression confirmed in 70–80% cervical cancer tumors by IHC; high H-score
TF validated as functional ADC target via tisotumab vedotin preclinical and clinical data
TF surface expression retained in recurrent/platinum-resistant disease
TF internalization kinetics characterized; MMAE payload compatible with TF biology
Normal tissue TF expression on vascular endothelium drives ocular and bleeding toxicity signal

trials.signal: Tisotumab vedotin (Tivdak) FDA approved September 2021 for recurrent cervical cancer 2L+
InnovaTV 204: TV ORR 24%, mDOR 8.3 months in heavily pretreated cervical cancer
InnovaTV 301: TV vs chemotherapy OS benefit confirmed (mOS 11.5 vs 9.5 months)
TV+pembrolizumab Phase III (InnovaTV 205) ongoing in 1L cervical
2 next-generation TF ADC programs (non-MMAE payloads) in IND/Phase I stage

regulatory.signal: Accelerated approval via ORR endpoint fully established in cervical cancer (TV precedent)
Confirmatory trial via OS endpoint feasible and confirmed (InnovaTV 301)
Differentiated safety profile vs TV (reduced ocular/bleeding) likely accepted as label differentiation
No CDx requirement for TF ADC — expression broadly high in indication

commercial.signal: Recurrent/metastatic cervical cancer US ~13,000 eligible patients/year at target line
High unmet need: limited options post-platinum + pembrolizumab; no strong 3L+ standard of care
TV pricing ~$15,000/month; market ~$400M US revenue (2023) — growing rapidly
Improved safety profile (reduced ocular/bleeding) is commercially meaningful — TV has significant toxicity management burden

literature.signal: Nectin-4 (PVRL4) expression in NSCLC: ~28% IHC 2+/3+ in adenocarcinoma
Nectin-4 expression in TNBC: ~32% IHC 2+/3+ with higher rates in basal-like
Nectin-4 expression in gastric cancer: ~25% IHC positive
EV (enfortumab vedotin) mechanism well-characterized: MMAE payload, strong internalization in urothelial
No prospective validation of Nectin-4 IHC as predictive biomarker outside urothelial context

trials.signal: Enfortumab vedotin FDA approved urothelial cancer: ORR 44%, mOS 12.9 months in 2L+
EV basket Phase II trial in Nectin-4+ solid tumors (non-urothelial) enrolling: NSCLC, TNBC, gastric cohorts
NSCLC EV single-arm Phase II (Astellas/Pfizer) interim ORR ~18% — lower than urothelial
Multiple companies pursuing Nectin-4 ADC expansion: consensus interest high
EV+pembro EV-302/KEYNOTE-869 approved in 1L urothelial — sets combination bar

regulatory.signal: Basket trial design feasible for Nectin-4+ solid tumors — FDA precedent from larotrectinib/entrectinib
Tumor-agnostic approval requires strong ORR signal + validated companion diagnostic
Indication-specific Phase III likely required for each tumor type unless basket signal very strong
EV peripheral neuropathy and hyperglycemia — safety monitoring requirements established

commercial.signal: NSCLC addressable Nectin-4+ population ~30,000 US/year; TNBC ~14,000; gastric ~10,000
High consensus attention — multiple companies pursuing identical expansion; reduced first-mover advantage
EV US revenue ~$1.2B (2023) and growing; expansion would significantly expand TAM if signal validates
Biosimilar/biobetter EV competition likely by 2030; window for expansion is 2025–2028 Phase III design
